reasoning:
The reference class is a +1% Elo increase for a FIDE chess Super-GM (which translates to gaining about 28 points, going from ~2754 to ~2782). The base rate constraint establishes this as incredibly rare (0.007). 

1. 2025-11-16: The Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page reflects the monthly FIDE lists identically, and only updates once a new list is released around the 1st of each month. By November 16, the data will still show the November 1 release rating, strictly rendering any gain impossible. (p=0.05, constrained by the 0.05 lower bound).

2. 2025-12-09: To reach ~2782 on the December list, Wei Yi would need to gain 28 points in a single rating period (likely by dominating the 2025 World Cup or similar events). At the 2750+ level, the K-factor is small, and drawing vs lower-rated players loses points. Gaining 28 points in one month at this tier is profoundly unlikely. (p=0.06, slightly increasing toward 0.5 for a wider horizon).

3. 2026-02-07: The February list gives him up to 3 rating periods (Nov, Dec, Jan with Tata Steel) to make the jump. Even so, 2782 would be more than 20 points above his all-time peak, representing a monumental break into the top 3-4 players globally. While there is more time and hence greater uncertainty than for the Dec date, it remains highly improbable. (p=0.08).
